start.speaker: Jambione
start.text: There you are. Come, son. Walk with me.

n1.speaker: Jambione
n1.text: See that stone? Your grandfather set it. His father before him. Each one marks where our land ends and the world begins.

n2.speaker: Jambione
n2.text: The Veil runs through them. You cannot see it — but you can feel it, if you are patient enough to listen.

choices.text: I want to learn the Veil.

learn_veil.speaker: Jambione
learn_veil.text: Good. That is the right answer. The Veil is not magic, Brochacho. It is attention. It is knowing your land so well that nothing can be taken from you without you feeling it first.

beyond.speaker: Jambione
beyond.text: The Settlement. Good people, mostly. But they have a way of making 'mostly' do a great deal of work.

task.speaker: Jambione
task.text:
Walk the boundary stones today. All seven. Tell me if any feel different than they did last week.

[i]Quest added: Walk the Boundary of Brochan Hold.[/i]

start.speaker: Jambione
start.text: The stones, Brochacho. Walk them first.

n1.speaker: Jambione
n1.text: He is not your enemy. Not yet. But a man who talks about 'offers' before he talks about the rain is a man who came to sell something.

n2.speaker: Jambione
n2.text: Do not let him make you feel clever for agreeing with him. That is how it starts.

start.speaker: Jambione
start.text: You felt it. The east stone.

n1.speaker: Jambione
n1.text: Someone moved it six inches north. Small enough to miss. Not small enough to be an accident.

n2.speaker: Jambione
n2.text: I will move it back. I will always move it back. And one day, when I am gone, that will be your job. Then Jesse's. Then whoever comes after Jesse.

n3.speaker: Jambione
n3.text: This land remembers every foot that walked it, son. It will forget the ones who do not belong. It never forgets ours.

start.speaker: Jesse
start.text: Finally! I have been out here all morning. Did Father send you to walk the boundary too?

n1.speaker: Jesse
n1.text: I found the east stone already. It is cold, Brochacho. Not morning-cold. The wrong kind of cold — like it has been touched.

show.speaker: Jesse
show.text: Put your hand on it. You will see. The Veil does not lie, even when people do.

dismiss.speaker: Jesse
dismiss.text: ...Fine. Walk past it yourself. You will feel it.

start.speaker: Jesse
start.text: The east stone. Go feel it yourself and then tell Father.

start.speaker: Elder Cassin
start.text: Ah — you must be one of Jambione's boys. I can see the resemblance. He has good sons.

n1.speaker: Elder Cassin
n1.text: My name is Cassin. I represent the Settlement Council. I am here to speak with your father — nothing serious. Neighbors talking.

choices.text: What do you want with him?

question.speaker: Elder Cassin
question.text: Ha — suspicious. Just like Jambione. That is not a bad thing, boy. The world rewards the careful.

fetch.speaker: Elder Cassin
fetch.text:
Tell him Elder Cassin sends his warmest regards. And that the offer still stands.

[i]It always will.[/i]

start.speaker: Elder Cassin
start.text: Is your father nearby? I would appreciate a word with him when he has a moment.

start.speaker: Jambione
start.text: You have done well today. The Hold knows you walked it.

n1.speaker: Jambione
n1.text: Cassin will come back. Men like him always do. When he does — remember what the east stone felt like. Hold that feeling.

n2.speaker: Jambione
n2.text: Now come inside. Your mother has supper waiting.
